original: You decide to bring out your Codex to snap a picture. Painted at the bottom, there is a horizontal rectangle with a tiny zaika script that your Codex translates as a big number sequence. Huh.

The alley’s tightness leaves little room for stepping back, but eventually you manage to get a good angle from the side, and the illusion is unraveled: the strokes depict a pile of guns on a background of fire. Duly impressed, you decide to snap a pic with your Codex. As you adjust the zoom, something jumps at you: at the bottom, there’s a sequence of small zaika characters written vertically that your Codex automatically translates as numbers.\n\nYou suppose it’s the artist’s signature?
Still looks pretty cool though, so you decide to snap a pic.

\n\nScrolling through the graffiti pictures in your Codex, you notice there is a certain stylistic similarity between them and decide to zoom in to check if the artist(s) left their name. Strangely, those pieces are all signed with different number sequences. Wait, doesn’t that sound a lot like something you learned in school?
That teacher was hot...

\n\nSuddenly, it hits you – those are geographic coordinates! Inputting them in your Codex, you mark the place on your map. It’s a wild guess, but who knows, maybe you could get to meet this graffiti artist.
Next
Following the coordinates from the graffiti clues, you wade through alley after alley.

\n\nThe planet’s natural illumination – or what little remains after passing through the smog-filled sky – is blotched out by the impossibly tall, dilapidated buildings that surround you on all sides. And yet, despite the oppressive atmosphere, the alley you presently find yourself in is brimming with noisy, chaotic life, brightly lit by a sea of storefronts that shine with neon letters, competing for attention.

\n\nThe denizens of this place, almost exclusively zaika, don’t seem particularly bothered by your presence – unlike the mob that attacked your transport car when you first arrived in Dhaal. Still, many cast opportunistic glances or treacherous smiles in your direction, inviting you to drop your guard just long enough for them to descend on you like vultures. Were you to disappear in a place like this, probably no-one would be able to find you.

\n\nEventually, you manage to locate the goal: a tiny set of downward stairs squeezed between the ‘smiling hydra’ and the ‘purple haze’, both of which figured in the graffiti art. The storefront – if it can be called that – consists of a nondescript metal door with no nameplate or other form of identification.

\n\nInspecting the door, you find a simple buzzer and ring it. A long moment passes, and you have a distinct feeling that someone is spying on you through a hidden camera, but can’t seem to find anything like that. Then, the door unlocks with a metallic clack, and dim light shows that the narrow stair continues downward, bending around a corner.

\n\nYou’ve barely taken three steps when you hear the clack of the door closing automatically behind you. For lack of options, you go down and soon reach what should be the store proper: a slightly wider space with a counter. That’s all. No shelves, displays, nothing, though you’re sure that can’t be the case. At least the place has proper illumination.
\n\n(New Codex entry unlocked: Zaika.)
Next

A practically invisible door slides open on the metallic wall behind the counter, and a tall zaika steps through, probably some 7' tall. The stranger, whom you suppose runs the place, has dark violet skin, a raspberry-pink mohawk and eyes of the same color. Her features toe the line of androgyny, with angular cheekbones, a delicate nose and a very toned build whose muscular curves complement her DD-cups beautifully.

\n\nThe perky mounds happen to be squeezed snugly by suspenders – if a measly pair of dark leather straps can even be called that. Her lower half is clad in dark trousers and military boots. The latex-like material of her pants molds closely to her form, delineating generous hips and thick legs that promise to be just as strong as the rest of her.

\n\nYou notice an almost invisible zipper at the side of her trousers, tempting you to just reach for it and pull it down. Meanwhile, the trademark tail of her species sways to and fro, though its genital ending is holographically disguised to look like a tapered tip.
\n\n“Hey there, sorry about the delay,” she says offhandedly, her voice a soft, velvety contralto. “I’m Rivet, you are?” You introduce yourself with
a sultry smile on your [pc.lipsChaste], giving your first name along with a suggestive wink.
a bashful smile and give her your name, trying not to gaze too directly.
a grunt and your first name, crossing your arms over your [pc.chest].
a friendly smile and give her your first name, extending a hand, which Rivet shakes with a firm grip.

\n\nShe smirks, “I must say I was surprised when I saw an offworlder outside. You spoiled scaredy-cats don’t usually come to the wild parts of town, at least not without an armed escort. Though, judging from your gear, I suppose you’re not just any offworlder,” she adds, her gaze sweeping you from head to toe.
\n\nYou confirm that you’re not just any traveler, you’re a rusher.

\n\n“A rusher? I’ve heard about your type – adventurers who brave the dangers of unexplored areas in search for riches and opportunity. I suppose you would need a place to acquire good gear and do business without having to worry about zaibatsu surveillance, am I right?”

\n\nYou nod, and her eyes scrutinize you for an uncomfortably long moment. When her pink eyes return to your [pc.eyeColor] ones, you notice the hint of an appreciative smile on her lips. “How did you find out about this place anyway?” she asks.
\n\nYou shrug and tell her you just followed clues from the graffiti scattered around the city.

\n\nThe amazon nods in appreciation – almost as if you’ve just passed a secret test. “Well, in that case...” she presses a button on her belt, and several sections of the wall slide open to reveal shelves with all sorts of gear. Some are easily recognizable, like guns, body armor and grenades. Others, not so much.
\n\n“So, what are we after? Or maybe you’re looking to offload some stuff instead?”

Rivet is a tall, imposing zaika who boasts a mix of feminine and masculine beauty. While her body is powerfully built, her proud face is marked by prominent cheekbones, a delicate nose and full lips that habitually hint at a confident smile. Her hair, a raspberry-pink mohawk, shares the hue of her eyes, creating a vibrant first impression. The zaika’s right ear bears a number of piercings, while the left one has been almost entirely replaced with cybernetics.

\n\nThe amazon’s right arm is also cybernetic, albeit only from the elbow down, but this hasn’t stopped her from working out her biceps. In fact, the curves of Rivet’s muscles – from traps to triceps to delts – are beautifully outlined on her dark-violet skin, complementing "+(rivet.biggestTitSize()>=5?"the bulk of her knockers":"her shapely tits")+" very well.

\n\nRivet’s upper body is ‘garbed’ by suspenders, if a measly pair of dark straps can be called that. The straps go over her shoulders and connect to a metallic tech-belt, squeezing her breasts snugly. The smooth, nippleless mounds are about [rivet.cupSize]s at present and additionally supported by taut ligaments that help them to stay proudly pert.

\n\nYou wonder if this choice of attire was made to respect (or mock) offworlder sensibilities, or if Rivet simply likes the look – it barely passes minimum modesty requirements of most U.G.C. planets. Incidentally, it also serves to frame her beefy six-pack.

\n\nThe amazon’s lower half is clad in dark trousers and military boots. The shiny, plastic-like material of her pants molds closely to her form, delineating generous hips and thick thighs that promise to be just as strong as the rest of her. You notice an almost invisible zipper at the side of Rivet’s trousers, constantly tempting you to pull it down.
\n\nMeanwhile, the trademark tail of her species sways to and fro, though its genital ending is holographically disguised to look like a tapered tip.
You tell Rivet you’d like to chat for a bit, if she’s not in a hurry.

\n\n“Sure, I don’t mind. You look like someone who’s got good stories to share. Come on in,” the amazon invites. As she turns around, a section of the wall slides open, leading you to her tiny-but-cozy flat. At a glance, the place comprises a living room connected to a side kitchenette, with a countertop in-between. At the far wall, there’s a door that likely leads to her bedroom.

\n\n“I’ll get us drinks, make yourself comfortable,” she says, indicating the small red sofa. Once you’re sat, your eyes take in the sights – though decoration is spartan, the flat’s pitch-black walls have been painted with a veritable mosaic of graffiti art in contrasting garish colors. It’s a bit daunting at first, but no less impressive for it.
\n\nUndertones of anti-establishment ideology are evident, though you can only decode a fraction of it, not being very familiar with zaika culture.

\n\nRivet returns a minute later with a pair of cyan drink pouches, and you immediately take a sip to clear your throat of all the smog you’ve breathed on the streets. The drink tastes mildly sweet, minty and alcoholic, though you can’t for the life of you imagine what it’s made from. Chemicals, for sure.
\n\n“So, what do you want to talk about?”

As you admire Rivet’s physique, it occurs to you that her tall, amazonian looks make her quite conspicuous. Isn’t that dangerous in her field?

\n\n“Well, yeah, if a Peer sees me there’s a chance they’ll take offense at my height and try to put me in my place. True story, by the way. But that was the reason I got those height mods in the first place, you know,” the smuggler shrugs.
\n\nPuzzled, you ask how one thing connects with the other.

\n\nRivet breathes an impatient sigh. “Well, I guess you’re new here, I can’t blame you for not knowing. Peers are the zaibatsu elite, and they take a lot of pride in the fact they’re taller. But that’s only because they grow up coddled in their families’ private paradises, while we lowborn are forced to breathe industrial smog from the moment we’re born and eat only cheap processed food.”

\n\nSo, basically what she’s saying is... that she got height mods just to rub it on their face? The amazon confirms with a nod, then you point to her cybernetic arm – is that somehow connected to her... subversive behavior?

\n\nRivet chuckles. “Oh, no, that was in a gang scrimmage. Some bastard shot my bike with a fucking railgun, and my arm got squashed in the crash. I left the gang after that – not crazy enough to trust my life to people who can’t cover my ass. This, though...” she trails off, pointing to her cybernetic ear. “Yeah, this was a Peer.”

\n\nYou look into her pink eyes expectantly, so she continues: “The bitch didn’t like it when I refused to be her whore, so she paid some mercs to rough me up. I bet she still has my ear in a jar somewhere,” Rivet laughs darkly. “But yeah, I got a little more careful after that. Should be fine as long as they don’t catch wind of my business.”
\n\nYou raise an eyebrow and comment that it would be an easy thing for the zaibatsu to monitor the area with drones.

\n\n“You’re right, and I wouldn’t be surprised if they even had a doll implanted in this community. But, those aren’t very smart, and an actual Peer would never set foot in this alley – it’s too ‘filthy’ for their refined tastes,” she finishes in a mocking tone.

\n\nSpeaking of which, why did she choose to run an underground smuggler? That strikes you as the kind of thing the zaibatsu wouldn’t take kindly to. Rivet’s face darkens for a moment, but she regains her cool
when you explain you’re a smuggler as well.
when you reassure her you’re no stranger to shady dealings.

\n\n“When the game is rigged, you get screwed if you try to play by the rules. After a few years, I couldn’t afford to get screwed anymore – not when there were people depending on me. And let’s leave it at that, alright?”

\n\nFair enough, you wouldn’t expect Rivet to tell you her whole life story just like that. But, putting on a friendly grin, you tell her there’s one last thing you’re just dying to know: the secret behind her stunning physique.

\n\nA cocky smirk appears on the amazon’s lips. “Flattery won’t get you everywhere, you know... but I would be lying if I said I didn’t enjoy it just now. The secret is discipline, baby,” she brags, flexing a bicep.
\n\nYou space out for a moment, admiring the bulgy muscle and the rest of her curves.

\n\n“Well, I told you a little about me, [pc.name], so why don’t you tell me a little about yourself, to make things even?” And, just like that, you spend the next several minutes talking about your homeworld and recounting some of the many odd encounters you’ve had so far.
Pulling out your Co

dex, you show Rivet your pictures of the graffiti clues and ask if she was the one who painted them – the artwork is really good! The amazon smiles heartily. “Oh, graffiti is a hobby of mine, been doing it for a few years now, though I’ve studied other painting styles before.”

\n\nSomewhat surprised, you ask if art education is normally... available to lower class zaika. For a second, she looks uncharacteristically flustered, but composes herself. “Oh, no, not at all. I had to go out of my way doing online research.”
\n\nNot entirely convinced, you press the amazon – one can’t get to her level by just reading about art. She must have had a lot of practice too.

\n\n“That, my friend, is where cyberspace comes in!” she says with a wink. “Granted, moving your hands in VR develops a different kind of dexterity than holding a pencil in real life – or so they say anyway, not like I would know – but it still teaches you technique. Some cyber artists are simply mind-blowing, I should take you to see their stuff one of these days.”
\n\nThis seems to be a topic Rivet is very enthusiastic about. Did she consider a career in cyber art?

\n\n“Hmm, yes, but it wouldn’t work. To reach that level in cyber art, you have to pretty much live in cyberspace, and I’m just not cut out for that. But more importantly, graffiti has more value in the real world. My art is an expression of nonconformity, it’s transgressive by nature and intent,” she muses.
\n\nYou nod, it looks like the roots of graffiti are similar everywhere.

\n\n“Well, it’s one of the few ways marginalized people can have artistic expression. I mean, do you think the Peers let us into mainstream media?” she scoffs. “Thankfully, my style only needs spray paint and walls – and that is something I can get my hands on pretty easily,” she adds with a cocky smirk.

\n\nHer mischievous enthusiasm is infectious, and you find yourself smiling back. But speaking of walls and marginalized people, what about local gangs? Don’t they paint over her stuff to mark territory?

\n\n“Not anymore,” she shrugs. “I’ve had a few problems with them in the past, but we’ve managed to settle our differences. They need my services – I need eyes and ears. It’s a win-win. Besides, they’ve started to respect me and my art, as I respect theirs. Mutual respect is important if you want to survive down here.”

\n\nYou nod slowly, wondering how deep the web of relations goes in the underworld. Rivet’s deep voice shakes you out of your thoughts: “Say, do you happen to like some kind of art? Music, movies... I’d be very interested to check some offworld art.”
\n\nAnd so, you cozy up to the amazon and spend the next several minutes showing stuff on your Codex.
You remark that she doesn’t seem very fond of the zaibatsu – sure, a lot of people seem to dislike them, but you’d like to hear Rivet’s personal take on the issue.
\n\nThe amazon sighs. “You know how the zaika from the zaibatsu are called Peers, and the others not? Well, what they’re really saying is ‘we are better than you’, and I think that’s bullshit.”

\n\nTrying to tease her a little, you purse your [pc.lipsChaste] in thought for a moment and try to reason that the big people aren’t all that bad. Like, they sell a lot of goodies to everyone, right?
\n\nTrying not to sound too confrontational, you ask her if hierarchy is really all that bad, after all the zaibatsu seem to be providing a lot of lifestyle improvements for the people.
\n\nTrying to goad her, you snort and reason that the people who are in power are there for a reason, and that the zaibatsu seem to be doing more good than harm, overall.
\n\nTrying to extract a better reply from her, you reason that hierarchy isn’t necessarily bad, and the zaibatsu seem to be providing a lot of lifestyle improvements for the masses.

\n\nRivet snorts in response. “Yeah, right. They fill the air with smog, dump toxic waste on the water and replace all natural grain with infertile seeds that only they can produce. Then they overcharge for medicine, potable water, food and everything else you can imagine. If you call that a ‘lifestyle improvement’, I seriously don’t know what the fuck is wrong with you!”
\n\nShe stops and lets out a tired sigh. “Sorry, that was uncalled for.” You reassure Rivet that it takes way more than that to offend you.

\n\n“You’re not from this planet, so it’s no surprise if you’ve really bought the crap they keep saying about ‘making life better for everyone’. What we lowborn get is just a trickle of the tech they have – this is their way of staying in power, you know?”
\n\nWell, you reason it makes sense, if what she’s saying is true. How do people manage to put food on the table?

\n\n“You’ve got only two options: work under one of the zaibatsu for a pathetic wage, or try to be an independent worker – and good luck with that, you’ll get snuffed out if you start to become a thorn in their side. Besides, they make the rules, so the game is rigged from the start.”
\n\nYou’ve got to admit that doesn’t sound too good. Even the U.G.C.’s megacorps aren’t that oppressive... probably... maybe.

\n\nThe amazon looks skeptical for a moment but shifts to a neutral face. “From the videos I’ve been watching, I’ll admit you offworlders do seem to be a bit better off. Anyway, I’m sure you’ve got a good grasp of my opinions now, but I suggest you see Dhaal with your own eyes and draw your own conclusions.”
\n\nYeah, that sounds like a good idea...

\n\nRivet’s shop is just to the west of here.

The remains of a wrecked vehicle sag across the curb more depressingly than a passed-out drunk. Its access panels are gone, every ounce of salvageable technology scavenged. Dents riddle the single intact fender so thoroughly that it looks like like it challenged a jackhammer to a boxing match. What’s left is buried under dozens of coats of luminescent paint. Gang symbols and juvenile scrawls of tail-mounted genitals war for dominance. Thus far, the genitals are winning.

\n\nThis street would be pretty unremarkable, were it not for the mosaics of garish gang graffiti coloring the walls. It’s even disorienting, if you stop to look at it. One, in particular, jumps at you: a depiction of a three-breasted zaika with a broad smile, almost as if she’s inviting you to unwind with her. Nice.
\n\nLife on Dhaal continues on to the north and south.
Snap Pic
This back alley is deader than the mushrooms hanging cooked onto the side of the closest heat vent. Misty vapors pour out around them, lending the area an otherworldly, insubstantial vibe.

\n\nAs you’re crossing this section, you notice an unusual graffiti on the nearest alley wall. At first, it looks like nothing more than a bunch of chaotic strokes in hues of red and black, but you can’t shake the feeling that you’re missing something. The situation brings to mind a painting your Dad showed you once – it only made sense when you looked at it from a distance.
Snap Pic

\n\nThis section is marked by an unusual alley graffiti that looks, at first, like nothing more than a bunch of chaotic strokes in hues of red and black. However, when observed from a distance, it actually depicts a pile of guns on a background of fire.
\n\nBusier streets lie north, while the alley twists east.

\n\nThis area has a large wall that has been almost completely filled with swirling purple strokes of graffiti art. In the middle, there are wobbly letters that your Codex translates as ‘Purple Haze’, and written in tiny script at the bottom is another set of letters that reads as ‘HIGH’ plus a number sequence. Yeah, you suppose whoever painted this must have been pretty high at the time.
